---
title: "Projects"
---

## Atlas of Urban Fragility

A comparative spatial analysis of four fragile cities, applying one consistent methodology to surface where essential services and population are misaligned.

### [Cox's Bazar — Rohingya Refugee Settlements](atlas/coxs-bazar.html)

Health facility density across the Kutupalong-Balukhali camp area. About 30% of the dense population sits in hexes with no nearby health facility. Services concentrate in the original camp core; the expansion periphery lags.

### [Kabul — Central Districts](atlas/kabul.html)

The pattern inverts. Roughly 74% of mapped buildings in central Kabul sit in hexes with no nearby health facility. Facilities form scattered clusters in a city that is dense almost everywhere, a case of service provision lagging behind rapid urbanization.

### [Mosul — Post-conflict Recovery](atlas/mosul.html)

A city rebuilding after the 2014-2017 conflict. The analysis surfaces the east-west divide across the Tigris: services have re-concentrated on the less-damaged side, while the Old City and the western neighborhoods continue to recover more slowly.

### [Goma — Conflict and Climate Convergence](atlas/goma.html)

A city sitting between Lake Kivu and Mount Nyiragongo, hosting waves of displacement from regional conflict. Health facilities concentrate in the formal city center; the IDP-hosting northern periphery shows the highest gap density.

---

## How this site is built

This portfolio is itself reproducible: written in Quarto, hosted via GitHub Pages, with code openly available on [GitHub](https://github.com/heissj/heissj.github.io).
